### Post by justleen on 2007-03-22
i think your trying to unzip a tarball..
try 
```

tar -zxvf xx.tgz 
```

how about ```
 sudo apt-get install gaim 
```?
Gaim works brilliantly (its acutally installed already on the standard ubuntu)
[http://gaim.sourceforge.net/screenshots.php](http://gaim.sourceforge.net/screenshots.php)

### Post by Bachstelze on 2007-03-22
AIM is now installed on your desktop, you can run it with

```
~/Desktop/usr/bin/aim
```

to install it system-wide, do

```
sudo tar xzvf file.tgz -C /
```
